zh.lifeSummary: 當Nancy Drew於1830年出生在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可劍橋大學默里 利默里克時，她的父親Samuel Lemuel Drew31歲，母親Olive Abigail Randall35歲。她於1854年4月11日與Hiram Colbroth在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可斯米納結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1860年住在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可且在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可盧德洛福住了大約10年。她去世於1902年1月11日在美國威斯康辛州索耶海達德享年72歲，埋葬在美國明尼蘇達州華盛頓斯利沃特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nancy Drew于1830年出生在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可劍橋大學默里 利默里克时，她的父亲Samuel Lemuel Drew31岁，母亲Olive Abigail Randall35岁。她于1854年4月11日与Hiram Colbroth在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可斯米納结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1860年住在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可且在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可盧德洛福住了大约10年。她去世于1902年1月11日在美国威斯康辛州索耶海達德享年72岁，埋葬在美国明尼苏达州華盛頓斯利沃特。

nameMeanings.name: Drew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from a Norman French personal name Dreus based on ancient Germanic Drogo, the name of one of the sons of Charlemagne (compare Gothic driugan ‘to serve as a soldier’). In Old French this name became Dreus, Drues (nominative) and Dru, Driu, Dreu (accusative) and was introduced to England by the Normans. For further information about this name, see also Montague .
English: from the personal name Drew, a pet form of Andrew which was confused with the same name of Old French origin (see 1 above).

Irish: when not an adoption of the English surname, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Druaidh or Ó Druaidh or Ó Draoi ‘son’ and ‘descendant of the druid’, from draoi ‘druid’, genitive druadh or draoi. All of these were often Anglicized as Drury .

variants.name: Drews
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerhard, Gunter, Hans, Heinz, Helmut, Otto, Reinhold, Bernd, Detlef, Egon, Erna.
English: variant of Drew with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also found in Guernsey in the Channel Islands.
Americanized form of German, Danish, and Dutch   Drewes  .

variants.name: Drewer
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Droart, Drouart (which is probably from ancient Germanic Droghard, with the first element as in Drew + hard ‘brave, hardy’).

variants.name: Drewett
meanings: English (Wiltshire, Berkshire, and Surrey): of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Druet, a diminutive of Drue, Dreu (from ancient Germanic Drogo); see Drew 1. Alternatively, the name may be from a diminutive of Old French dru ‘lover’; see Drew 4.

variants.name: Dronet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcee, Desire, Olympe.

French (mainly Loire-Atlantique): from a diminutive of the personal name Dron, derived from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ).

variants.name: Droz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Aida, Cruz Maria, Diego, Jose, Miguel, Milagros, Nydia, Osvaldo, Rodriguez, Vidal.

French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). This surname is also found in Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Drea
meanings: Irish (Kilkenny): shortened Anglicized form of Ó Draoi (see Drew ).

variants.name: Montague
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either Montaigu-le-bois (Manche) or Montaigu (Manche, Aisne), named with Old French mont ‘hill’ + agu ‘pointed’ (from Latin acutus). Drogo de Montagud was a companion of William the Conqueror and founder of the Montagu(e) family, among whom the personal name Drogo was revived in the 19th century. Another family of the name Monatgu have held the titles of Duke of Montagu, Earl of Sandwich, Earl and Duke of Manchester, Earl of Halifax, and Earl of Beaulieu, and.
Irish: adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Taidhg (see McTigue ).

variants.name: Drouin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Benoit, Jacques, Andre, Clermont, Emile, Gaston, Gilles, Ovide, Pierre, Serge.

French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Droue, a variant of Dreue, which is from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). Compare Derouen , Derouin , and Tron .

variants.name: Drury
meanings: Anglo-Norman: Druerie, courtship, gallantry, love

variants.name: Drouhard
meanings: French:
from the ancient Germanic personal name Droghard, composed of the elements drog (presumably meaning ‘battle’; see also Drew ) and hard ‘hard, strong’.
alternatively, a derivative of the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names like Droghard (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Nancy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nancie
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nanci
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.
Historical Boundaries: 1839: Aroostock, Maine, United States
Historical Boundaries 1849: Minnesota Territory, United States 1858: Minnesota, United States

historicEvents.title: First State to Attempt Prohibition
historicEvents.description: "In 1851, Maine outlawed the sale of alcohol, allowing exceptions only for ""medicinal, mechanical, and manufacturing purposes"". This made Maine the first state to experiment with prohibition. Neal Dow, mayor of Portland, believed that alcohol was linked to slavery and was also convinced by the Christian temperance movement. Dow ran into problems later for his anti-immigration rhetoric against the Irish, and also for breaking his own prohibition laws; although not a designated ""purchaser"", Dow personally purchased alcohol to distribute to local doctors, violating a technicality. As the citizens turned against him, Dow eventually ordered soldiers to fire on protesters. This marked a sharp decline in Dow's political career, and the Maine Law was repealed by 1856. Aspects of the law would remain in tact, however, and ultimately paved the way for the 18th Amendment, which prohibited alcohol on the national level."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Smithsonian - Why Was Maine the First State to Try Prohibition?

historicEvents.title: Sorry Mr. President, You can't do that.
historicEvents.description: This Act was to restrict the power of the President removing certain office holders without approval of the Senate. It denies the President the power to remove any executive officer who had been appointed by the president with the advice and consent of the Senate, unless the Senate approved the removal during the next full session of Congress. The Amendment was later repealed.

historicEvents.title: The First Successful Typewriter is Invented
historicEvents.description: A patent was filed on October 11, 1867, on a new direct action typewriter. The patent was filed by Christopher Latham Sholes, Carlos Glidden, and Samuel Soule who had invented the prototype in Milwaukee.

historicEvents.title: Sinking of USS Maine
historicEvents.description: During the Cuban War for Independence, the USS Maine had been sent to Havana to look out for the interests of the United States. On February 15, 1898, at approximately 21:40, an explosion on USS Maine was caused by roughly 5 long tons of powder. The front of the ship was demolished and the remains quickly sunk to the bottom of the ocean. 260 men lost their lives in the explosion itself, with at least six more dying afterwards from related injuries. The event kicked off an atmosphere of contention that would eventually lead to the Spanish-American War.

en.lifeSummary: When Nancy Drew was born in 1830, in New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United States, her father, Samuel Lemuel Drew, was 31 and her mother, Olive Abigail Randall, was 35. She married Hiram Colbroth on 11 April 1854, in Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She lived in Aroostook, Maine, United States in 1860 and Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, United States for about 10 years. She died on 11 January 1902, in Hayward, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United States, at the age of 72, and was buried in Stillwater, Washington, Minnesota, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nancy Drew est née en 1830, à New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis, son père, Samuel Lemuel Drew, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Olive Abigail Randall, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Hiram Colbroth le 11 avril 1854, à Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle a vécu à Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis en 1860 et à Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis pendant environ 10 ans. Elle est décédée le 11 janvier 1902, à Hayward, Sawyer, Wisconsin, États-Unis, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Stillwater, Washington, Minnesota, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nancy Drew 1830 in New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Samuel Lemuel Drew 31 und ihre Mutter Olive Abigail Randall 35. Sie hat am 11. April 1854 in Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten Hiram Colbroth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1860 hat sie in Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt, und ungefähr 10 Jahre lang hat sie in Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 11. Januar 1902 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Hayward, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Stillwater, Washington, Minnesota, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nancy Drew è nata il nel 1830 nel New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Samuel Lemuel Drew, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Olive Abigail Randall, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Hiram Colbroth il 11 aprile 1854, nel Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1860 e nel Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 10 anni. Morì il 11 gennaio 1902, nel Hayward, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolta a Stillwater, Washington, Minnesota, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Nancy Drewが1830年にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクニュー リメリックで生まれた時，父Samuel Lemuel Drewは31歳，母Olive Abigail Randallは35歳であった。1854年4月11日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクスムイルナでHiram Colbrothと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1860年にはアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクに，アメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクルドロウに約10年間住んでいた。彼女は1902年1月11日にアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州サウヤーヘイワードで72歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ミネソタ州ワシュイントンスティルワターにて埋葬された。

ko.South Berwick, York, Maine, United State: 미국 메인 주 요르크 소우스 버윅
ko.Pine City, Pine, Minnesota, United State: 미국 미네소타 주 핀 핀 시티

ko.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United S: 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭
ko.lifeSummary: Nancy Drew(이)가 1830년 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Samuel Lemuel Drew(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Olive Abigail Randall(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1854년 4월 11일 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 스미른아에서 Hiram Colbroth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1860년도에 미국 메인 주 아루스투크에서, 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 루들로에서 약 10년 동안 살았다. 그녀는 1902년 1월 11일 미국 위스콘신 주 사w예르 헤이워드에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 미네소타 주 와신그턴 스틸왓어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nancy Drew nasceu em 1830, em New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Samuel Lemuel Drew, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Olive Abigail Randall, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Hiram Colbroth em 11 de abril de 1854, em Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ela viveu em Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América em 1860 e em Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América por quase 10 anos. Ela faleceu em 11 de janeiro de 1902, em Hayward, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Stillwater, Washington, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nancy Drew родилась в 1830 г. в местности ню лимирикк, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Samuel Lemuel Drew было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Olive Abigail Randall, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hiram Colbroth 11 апреля 1854 г. в местности смирна, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она жила в местности арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году и в местности лудлоу, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты около 10 лет. Она умерла 11 января 1902 г. в местности хеивард, савиир, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности стиллвэитир, вашингтон, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nancy Drew nació en 1830, en New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos, su papá, Samuel Lemuel Drew, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Olive Abigail Randall, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Hiram Colbroth el 11 de abril de 1854, en Smyrna, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos en 1860 y en Ludlow, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos durante unos 10 años. Murió el 11 de enero de 1902, en Hayward, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Stillwater, Washington, Minnesota, Estados Unidos.
